reasoning: Prelim data from Feb 2025 shows the 2024 fertility rate rose to 0.75, easily beating the 2023 baseline of 0.72. More importantly, South Korea experienced a 14.9% surge in marriages in 2024, the largest increase since 1970. Since marriages are a very strong leading indicator for births (with 35% of babies born to parents married for under 2 years), this surge practically guarantees that the 2025 fertility rate will also be elevated, likely exceeding 2024's rate and certainly remaining well above 2023's 0.72. The South Korean government has officially projected a further increase to 0.79 in 2025. Therefore, the probability of 2025's fertility rate being higher than 2023's is extremely high.
